vulnerabilities.description: Unsafe array splice with indexOf. If '--remote-debugging-port=0' is not found, indexOf returns -1, causing splice(1, -1) to remove all but the last element unexpectedly. This could corrupt process.argv and cause unexpected behavior.

Untrusted file read from user data directory (DevToolsActivePort). While port validation exists, a malicious local user could potentially hijack the DevToolsActivePort file to redirect connections to an attacker-controlled service.
Socket path created in daemon directory without explicit ownership or permission verification. Local privilege escalation possible if daemon directory permissions are misconfigured.
Environment variable PW_INSTRUMENT_MODULES enables code instrumentation without explicit validation. Could potentially be abused if environment is untrusted.

recommendations: Fix the array splice logic in loader.js: use 'const idx = process.argv.indexOf(...); if (idx !== -1) { process.argv.splice(...) }' to safely handle missing strings
Add file ownership verification when reading DevToolsActivePort and other sensitive local files
Implement explicit socket file permission checks (0600) when creating daemon socket paths
Document and validate all environment variables that control security-relevant behavior
Consider restricting environment variable-based instrumentation to development builds only
Implement symlink attack protection when creating/reading files in user home directories
Add input validation for port numbers read from files before attempting connections
